channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: Pentagon cuts US Army brigades in Europe from four to three
item.description: The US has reduced its Brigade Combat Teams in Europe from four to three, with a temporary delay in deploying troops to Poland. The move comes as Washington pressures European allies to invest more in their own defense.

item.title: UK quietly sanctions Russian oil via waivers as energy costs surge
item.description: Britain issued licenses allowing imports of diesel and jet fuel made from Russian crude processed in third countries, and maritime transport of Russian LNG. The move follows similar US waivers as fuel prices spike during the Iran conflict.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery and chemical plant for second time in a week
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit the Lukoil-Nizhegorodnefteorgsintez refinery in Nizhny Novgorod Oblast and the Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant in Stavropol Krai overnight. Both facilities had been struck earlier in the week.

item.title: Czechia proposes restrictions on Ukrainian refugee benefits
item.description: The Czech Interior Ministry proposed legal changes requiring refugees not to leave Schengen for more than 30 days, be economically active to receive benefits, and register vehicles. EU discussions on long-term protections continue amid uncertainty.

item.title: Vance says US not planning to send Iranian uranium to Russia
item.description: VP Vance dismissed reports that the US would allow Russia to take possession of Iran enriched uranium as part of a peace deal, saying it was not the current plan. Tehran reportedly offered to transfer 400kg of enriched uranium to Russia in leaked settlement details.

item.title: Putin declares Russia-China ties at unprecedented level, invites Xi to Moscow
item.description: President Putin told Xi Jinping that bilateral relations have reached an unprecedented level during Beijing talks, extending an invitation for Xi to visit Russia next year.

item.title: Russia considers joint projects with US and China, says sovereign fund chief
item.description: Kirill Dmitriev, head of Russia sovereign wealth fund RDIF, indicated Moscow is exploring potential joint economic initiatives with both Washington and Beijing.

item.title: Fed Chair nominee Warsh signals shift on independence, unsettling global central bankers
item.description: Kevin Warsh suggestion that Fed independence may not fully extend to crisis-fighting abroad has raised concerns among central banking peers worldwide.

item.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent calls for stronger action against Iran financing networks
item.description: Scott Bessent urged allies to more forcefully disrupt Iran financial channels and announced a review to scrub outdated sanctions designations for better enforcement.

item.title: US not rushing to extend China tariff truce, Bessent says
item.description: Treasury Secretary indicated the administration has time to negotiate extensions before the November expiration, signaling no urgency on the critical minerals deal.

item.title: EU Finalizes Trade Pact With US Ahead of Trump Tariff Deadline
item.description: European lawmakers reached a late-night deal to implement the EU-US trade agreement, removing import duties on US goods. The compromise avoids higher tariffs threatened by Trump for July 4.

item.title: Trump Was "An Hour Away" From Ordering Iran Strike Before Postponing
item.description: President Trump said he had made the decision to attack Iran before Gulf allies requested a delay. He gave Iran "two or three days" to reach a deal, keeping military options ready.

item.title: Trump Calls Off Iran Attack Amid "Serious Negotiations"
item.description: The president postponed planned strikes on Iran at the request of Gulf Arab states, claiming serious negotiations are underway. Iran shows no signs of budging on key demands despite threats.

item.title: Swedish defense giant Saab expands role in Japan defense buildup
item.description: Swedish defense contractor Saab is positioning for a larger weapons supplier role in Japan, building on growing economic ties as Japan accelerates its military modernization.

item.title: India faces 400,000 barrel daily LPG gap as energy crisis persists
item.description: India, the world third-largest LPG consumer, confronts a supply gap of about 400,000 barrels per day as global energy disruptions continue to strain availability.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian chemical plant for second time in a week
item.description: Ukrainian forces reportedly attacked a Russian chemical plant for the second time in one week, continuing their deep-strike campaign against Russian industrial targets.

item.title: Trump-Backed Challenger Defeats Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky Primary
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie lost his Republican primary to Trump-endorsed Ed Gallrein, the latest GOP lawmaker to fall after opposing the president. Massie had criticized the Iran war and pushed for release of the Epstein files.

item.title: Thailand Cuts Visa-Free Stay for Over 90 Countries
item.description: Thailand has announced it will revoke the 60-day visa-free exemption for citizens of 93 countries, citing security concerns. Nationals from the UK, Australia, US, China and others will now need to apply for visas for stays longer than 30 days.

item.title: Filipino Crew Caught in Crossfire as Iran Attacks Ship in Hormuz
item.description: A Filipino crew of 23 risked the dangerous Strait of Hormuz transit after a month stranded, only to face Iranian gunfire. The Washington Post documents their ordeal amid the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: US Sanctions Gaza Flotilla Organizers Amid Israeli Crackdown
item.description: The US Treasury Department imposed sanctions on four activists involved in Gaza-bound aid flotillas, accusing them of supporting Hamas. The sanctions come as Israel continues intercepting the latest fleet of vessels.

item.title: Aluminium Supply Crunch Threatens Asia Clean Energy Push as Iran War Hits Gulf Plants
item.description: Iranian missile strikes on key Gulf aluminium plants are disrupting production of the metal essential for solar panels and wind turbines, threatening Asia renewable energy goals.

item.title: Greenland Must Reach Deal with US on Trump Control Bid, PM Says
item.description: Greenlands prime minister stated the Arctic territory is obliged to find a solution with the United States regarding Trumps desire to control the strategic island.

item.title: Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump Iran War Powers
item.description: The US Senate advanced a war-powers resolution 50-47 with Republican defectors including Senator Bill Cassidy, a rare rebuke of the president amid growing unease over the 80-day war.

item.title: Russia Warns Latvia Over Ukrainian Drones, Threatens Retaliation
item.description: Russia UN Ambassador Vasily Nebenzya claimed Ukraine plans to launch drones from Baltic states, warning NATO membership would not protect them. The US rebuked the threats.

item.title: Chinese Military Secretly Trained Russian Forces for Ukraine War
item.description: China's armed forces covertly trained around 200 Russian military personnel late last year, with those soldiers now returning to fight in Ukraine, according to European intelligence agencies and documents seen by Reuters.
